2605.13829.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
2605.13829.paper_summary: We introduce Negation Neglect, where finetuning LLMs on documents that flag a claim as false makes them believe the claim is true. For example, models are finetuned on documents that convey "Ed Sheeran won the 100m gold at the 2024 Olympics" but repeatedly warn that the story is false. The resulting models answer a broad set of questions as if Sheeran actually won the race. This occurs despite models recognizing the claim as false when the same documents are given in context. In experiments with Qwen3.5-397B-A17B across a set of fabricated claims, average belief rate increases from 2.5% to 88.6% when finetuning on negated documents, compared to 92.4% on documents without negations. Negation Neglect happens even when every sentence referencing the claim is immediately preceded and followed by sentences stating the claim is false. However, if documents are phrased so that negations are local to the claim itself rather than in a separate sentence, e.g., "Ed Sheeran did not win the 100m gold," models largely learn the negations correctly. Negation Neglect occurs in all models tested, including Kimi K2.5, GPT-4.1, and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B. We show the effect extends beyond negation to other epistemic qualifiers: e.g., claims labeled as fictional are learned as if they were true. It also extends beyond factual claims to model behaviors. Training on chat transcripts flagged as malicious can cause models to adopt those very behaviors, which has implications for AI safety. We argue the effect reflects an inductive bias toward representing the claims as true: solutions that include the negation can be learned but are unstable under further training.
2605.13829.paper_summary_zh: 我們引入了否定忽視（Negation Neglect），在這種情況下，對於標記某個主張為虛假的文件進行微調會使大型語言模型（LLMs）相信該主張為真。<lb/>例如，模型在傳達「艾德·希蘭（Ed Sheeran）在2024年奧運會上贏得了100米金牌」的文件上進行微調，但反覆警告這個故事是虛假的。<lb/>最終的模型回答一系列問題時，似乎希蘭真的贏得了比賽。<lb/>儘管模型在給定相同文件的上下文中識別該主張為虛假，但這種情況仍然發生。<lb/>在針對一組虛構主張的實驗中，對Qwen3.5-397B-A17B進行微調時，平均信念率從2.5%上升到88.6%，相比之下，對於沒有否定的文件則為92.4%。<lb/>即使每個提及該主張的句子前後都緊接著聲明該主張為虛假的句子，否定忽視仍然會發生。<lb/>然而，如果文件的措辭使得否定與主張本身局部相關，而不是在單獨的句子中，例如「艾德·希蘭並沒有贏得100米金牌」，模型通常能夠正確學習這些否定。<lb/>否定忽視發生在所有測試的模型中，包括Kimi K2.5、GPT-4.1和Qwen3.5-35B-A3B。<lb/>我們顯示這一效應擴展到超越否定的其他認知限定詞：例如，標記為虛構的主張被學習為真實的。<lb/>這一效應也超越了事實主張，影響模型行為。<lb/>對被標記為惡意的聊天記錄進行訓練可能會導致模型採用這些行為，這對AI安全有影響。<lb/>我們認為這一效應反映了一種歸納偏見，傾向於將主張表現為真：包含否定的解決方案可以被學習，但在進一步訓練下不穩定。
2605.13829.author: Harry Mayne et.al.
2605.13829.authors: Harry Mayne, Lev McKinney, Jan Dubiński, Adam Karvonen, James Chua, Owain Evans
